prompt: A HomeCareOps platform capable of running an offline home-service marketplace and knowledge hub lets Customers browse a service catalog in a React web app, filter by category and tags (e.g., deep cleaning, handyman, senior support), and configure specifications such as visit duration (30/60/90 minutes), required headcount (1–4 techs), tools supplied by provider vs. customer, and add-ons like “inside fridge” or “same-day priority.” The service detail page supports multi-spec and bundle selection with real-time validation of price and capacity before checkout, including travel fees by service zone (e.g., $0 within 10 miles, $15 for 10–20 miles, and “not serviceable” beyond 20 miles) and a sales-tax toggle for jurisdictions that require it. Customers can favorite and compare up to 5 services side-by-side, review Q&A and prior verified reviews, and submit an order request only when the selected date/time slot remains available. After completion, Customers can post a review within 14 days, including a 1–5 rating, predefined tags, text, and up to 6 images (10 MB each), while a unified in-app inbox shows scheduled announcements (e.g., holiday hours) and role-targeted messages with read/unread state. When issues arise, Customers can open a complaint ticket from the order, attach evidence, track SLA countdowns, and receive outcome documentation once resolved; Administrators, Service Managers, and Moderators operate separate consoles for catalog setup, content publishing, and dispute handling.The backend uses Express to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the React frontend over the local network (or localhost), with MongoDB as the system of record for users, service definitions, pricing rules, capacity calendars, orders, reviews, tickets, messages, and content versions. Pricing is computed server-side with deterministic rules (base price + tiered duration pricing + surcharges such as 1.5× for after-hours 7:00 PM–7:00 AM, and +$25 for “same-day” when less than 4 hours’ notice), and capacity is enforced with atomic checks so a slot cannot be oversold; invalidated cart selections must return actionable errors and suggested alternatives. Reviews are anti-fraud gated to “verified purchase only,” limited to one review per order, and pass local moderation rules that block disallowed file types, reject images failing basic header validation, and quarantine content containing configured sensitive terms until a Moderator approves it. Complaint tickets route by category with deadlines (first response within 8 business hours, resolution target within 5 business days) and immutable outcome archiving. Media assets are stored on local disk via an Express static file server (no cloud), with SHA-256 deduplication, reference counting, and optional cropping/compression performed locally; rich text content supports draft-to-publish, scheduled publishing, rollback to any prior version, and dependency integrity checks to prevent deleting assets still referenced. Search uses MongoDB text indexes with incremental updates on create/update and a weekly cleanup job to remove orphaned index entries. Security includes username/password login only (minimum 12 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes), TLS for in-transit protection on the LAN, AES-256 encryption for sensitive fields at rest, masking of phone/address on non-privileged views, rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute/user), anomaly flags for new-device logins, configurable blacklists, comprehensive audit logs, and retention policies such as deleting closed ticket attachments after 365 days unless legally held.
